questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following statements about friction is INCORRECT?

options.A: Kinetic friction is less than maximum static friction
options.B: Rolling friction is much less than sliding friction
options.C: Coefficient of kinetic friction μk > coefficient of static friction μs
options.D: Friction acts tangentially to the surfaces in contact

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.8: Friction
questions.ncert_line: Static friction acts up to a maximum value (fs ≤ μs N). Kinetic friction fk = μk N is constant and less than maximum static friction (μk < μs). Rolling friction is much smaller than sliding friction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is 2MR²/5.
Statement II: The moment of inertia of a hollow sphere (thin shell) about its diameter is 2MR²/3.

options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — Systems of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6–7.7
questions.ncert_line: Torque τ = r × F = Iα. Angular momentum L = Iω. The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/5; a ring about its axis = MR²; a disc about its axis = MR²/2; a hollow sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a geostationary satellite?

options.B: It orbits in the equatorial plane
options.C: It can be at any altitude above the Earth
options.D: It appears stationary relative to a point on Earth

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.7: Earth Satellites
questions.ncert_line: For a satellite in a circular orbit at height h above Earth's surface: orbital velocity v₀ = √(GM/(R+h)). The time period T = 2π(R+h)/v₀. For h << R, v₀ ≈ √(gR) ≈ 7.9 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between source temperature T₁ = 800 K and sink temperature T₂ = 400 K is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.9: Carnot Engine
questions.ncert_line: The efficiency of a Carnot engine η = 1 − T₂/T₁, where T₁ is the temperature of the source and T₂ is the temperature of the sink. No engine can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: No heat engine operating between two temperatures can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.
Statement II: The efficiency of a Carnot engine can be 100% if T₂ = 0 K.

options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is practically impossible (Third Law prevents T₂ = 0)
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.9: Carnot Engine
questions.ncert_line: The efficiency of a Carnot engine η = 1 − T₂/T₁, where T₁ is the temperature of the source and T₂ is the temperature of the sink. No engine can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two tuning forks of frequencies 512 Hz and 516 Hz are sounded simultaneously. The number of beats per second is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.7: Beats
questions.ncert_line: When two sources of slightly different frequencies f₁ and f₂ are superimposed, beats are heard. Beat frequency = |f₁ − f₂|. Maximum beats per second that the human ear can distinguish is about 10.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plates has capacitance C. If a dielectric slab (K = 3) completely fills the space, the new capacitance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.9–2.10
questions.ncert_line: Capacitance C = Q/V. For a parallel plate capacitor C = ε₀A/d (no dielectric) or C = Kε₀A/d (with dielectric). Energy stored in a capacitor U = Q²/2C = CV²/2 = QV/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about the magnetic force on a moving charge?

options.A: It is always perpendicular to the velocity
options.B: It does positive work on the charge
options.C: It cannot change the speed of the charge
options.D: Its direction is given by F = qv × B

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.2: Magnetic Force
questions.ncert_line: The magnetic force on a moving charge: F = qv × B = qvB sinθ. This force is always perpendicular to both v and B, so it does no work on the charge and cannot change kinetic energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The cyclotron frequency (f = qB/2πm) is independent of:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.5: Cyclotron
questions.ncert_line: In a cyclotron, the radius of circular motion r = mv/qB. The cyclotron frequency f = qB/2πm — it is independent of the speed and radius (for non-relativistic speeds). Cyclotrons cannot accelerate electrons (too light, quickly become relativistic).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a transformer?

options.A: It works on the principle of mutual induction
options.B: It can work with both AC and DC
options.C: In a step-up transformer, Vs > Vp
options.D: An ideal transformer conserves power (Pin = Pout)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.10: Transformer
questions.ncert_line: For an ideal transformer: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np = Ip/Is. A step-up transformer increases voltage but decreases current proportionally (power is conserved). Transformers work only with AC, not DC.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two thin lenses of focal lengths f₁ = 20 cm and f₂ = −30 cm are placed in contact. The equivalent focal length is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.6: Thin Lenses
questions.ncert_line: Lens formula: 1/v − 1/u = 1/f. Lensmaker's equation: 1/f = (n−1)(1/R₁ − 1/R₂). Power P = 1/f (in dioptres when f is in metres). For thin lenses in contact: 1/f = 1/f₁ + 1/f₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Young's double slit experiment, the fringe width β = λD/d. If λ = 600 nm, D = 1 m, and d = 0.2 mm, the fringe width is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.3: Young's Double Slit
questions.ncert_line: In Young's double slit experiment, fringe width β = λD/d, where λ is wavelength, D is slit-to-screen distance, and d is slit separation. Constructive interference (bright fringe): path difference = nλ. Destructive: (n + ½)λ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: In the photoelectric effect, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.A: Increasing the intensity increases the number of emitted electrons
options.B: Kinetic energy of photoelectrons increases with frequency of light
options.C: There is a minimum threshold frequency below which no emission occurs
options.D: Increasing intensity of light increases the maximum KE of photoelectrons

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation, Section 11.4: Photoelectric Effect
questions.ncert_line: The work function (φ) is the minimum energy need to eject a photoelectron from a metal surface. Threshold frequency ν₀ = φ/h. The stopping potential V₀ is related to maximum KE by eV₀ = hν − φ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A photon is emitted when a hydrogen atom transitions from n=3 to n=2. This emission corresponds to which spectral series?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.4: Hydrogen Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: Hydrogen spectrum series: Lyman series (UV, n→1), Balmer series (visible, n→2), Paschen series (IR, n→3), Brackett (IR, n→4), Pfund (IR, n→5). The Rydberg formula: 1/λ = R(1/n₁² − 1/n₂²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At absolute zero (0 K), a pure semiconductor behaves like a/an:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.4–14.5
questions.ncert_line: In intrinsic semiconductors, electrons and holes are always equal (ne = nh). In n-type (donor impurity), electrons are majority carriers. In p-type (acceptor impurity), holes are majority carriers. At 0 K, semiconductors behave as insulators.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 5 kg is acted upon by two perpendicular forces 8 N and 6 N. The resultant acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F_net = 10 N, a = 10/5 = 2 m/s².
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: Resultant F = √(8²+6²) = 10 N. a = F/m = 10/5 = 2 m/s².

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.4

questions.question: Two capacitors 3 μF and 6 μF in series. Equivalent capacitance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 1/C_eq = 1/3 + 1/6 = 1/2. C_eq = 2 μF.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.14

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Three resistors 2Ω, 3Ω and 6Ω in parallel. Equivalent resistance is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.10

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A convex lens (f = 20 cm) forms a real image at 60 cm. Object distance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Lens formula gives u = −30 cm (30 cm from lens).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In YDSE with slits 0.5 mm apart, screen 1 m away, λ = 500 nm. Fringe width is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. β = λD/d = 500e-9 × 1 / 5e-4 = 1 mm.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is thrown horizontally from a height of 20 m. Time to reach the ground is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. h = ½gt² → t = √(2h/g) = √(40/10) = 2 s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: h = ½gt² → 20 = ½ × 10 × t² → t² = 4 → t = 2 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body starts from rest and accelerates uniformly at 4 m/s². Distance covered in the 5th second is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Distance in nth second: s_n = u + a(2n−1)/2 = 0 + 4(9)/2 = 18 m.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.4

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rms value of an AC voltage V = V₀ sinωt is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Vrms = V₀/√2. Similarly Irms = I₀/√2.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.2
questions.ncert_line: Vrms = V0/√2 = 0.707 V0. For household supply: 230 V rms means peak voltage = 230√2 ≈ 325 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A concave mirror of focal length 15 cm forms an image at infinity. The object is at:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Image at infinity → object at focus. u = f = 15 cm.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: For concave mirror: image at infinity when object is at focus (u = f = 15 cm).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: The work function of a metal is 2 eV. The threshold frequency for photoelectric emission is: (h = 6.6×10⁻³⁴ J·s)
questions.explanation: Correct: A. ν₀ = φ/h = 2eV/h = 3.2×10⁻¹⁹/6.6×10⁻³⁴ ≈ 4.8×10¹⁴ Hz.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation, Section 11.3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A projectile is launched at 60° to horizontal with speed 20 m/s. Maximum height reached is: (g=10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. H = u²sin²θ/2g = 400×0.75/20 = 15 m.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A cell of EMF 2V and internal resistance 0.5Ω is connected to an external resistance of 1.5Ω. Current in circuit is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. I = E/(R+r) = 2/2 = 1 A.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 10 kg block rests on a frictionless surface. A 100 N force is applied. The acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. a = F/m = 100/10 = 10 m/s².
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of 100 turns has flux change of 0.05 Wb in 0.1 s. The induced EMF is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. EMF = N × ΔΦ/Δt = 100 × 0.5 = 50 V.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.4

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 2 kg on a frictionless surface is connected to a spring (k = 200 N/m). If compressed 10 cm and released, maximum speed of block is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Energy conservation: ½mv² = ½kx². v = x√(k/m) = 0.1√100 = 1 m/s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.4

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of 100 turns has flux change of 0.02 Wb in 0.1 s. The induced EMF is:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. EMF = N × ΔΦ/Δt = 100 × 0.02/0.1 = 20 V.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a single slit diffraction pattern, the width of central maximum is 2λD/a. If slit width a is halved:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Width = 2λD/a ∝ 1/a. Halving a → width doubles. Narrower slit → broader diffraction.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Width ∝ 1/a. Halving a doubles the central maximum width.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In hydrogen atom, the wavelength of emitted radiation when electron jumps from n=3 to n=2 is (Hα line):
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Hα = 656 nm (red). Hβ = 486 nm (blue-green). Both in visible Balmer series.
questions.ncert_line: Hα line (3→2 transition, Balmer series) has wavelength 656.3 nm — red visible light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hydraulic lift has pistons of cross-sectional area 10 cm² and 500 cm². Force applied on small piston is 20 N. Load lifted is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F₂ = 20 × (500/10) = 1000 N. Hydraulic machines multiply force.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². The angular acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. α = τ/I = 10/2 = 5 rad/s². (Rotational analogue of F=ma.)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — System of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6
questions.ncert_line: τ = Iα → α = τ/I = 10/2 = 5 rad/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg block slides down a frictionless incline of height 5 m. Its speed at the bottom is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. v = √(2gh) = √(100) = 10 m/s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Energy conservation: mgh = ½mv² → v = √(2gh) = √(2×10×5) = √100 = 10 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A train 100 m long travelling at 72 km/h crosses a bridge 1 km long. Time to cross completely is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Distance = bridge + train length = 1100 m. Time = 1100/20 = 55 s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Speed = 72 km/h = 20 m/s. Total distance = 1000 + 100 = 1100 m. Time = 1100/20 = 55 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 5 kg on a frictionless surface is pulled by a force of 15 N. The acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. a = F/m = 15/5 = 3 m/s².
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: F = ma → a = F/m = 15/5 = 3 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 10 m/s collides with a wall and bounces back at 8 m/s. Change in momentum is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. |Δp| = m|v_f − v_i| = 2 × 18 = 36 kg m/s (taking original direction as positive).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.6
questions.ncert_line: Δp = m(v_f − v_i) = 2 × (−8 − 10) = 2 × (−18) = −36 kg m/s. Magnitude = 36 kg m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The energy of hydrogen atom in the ground state (n=1) is −13.6 eV. Energy in n=3 state is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. En = −13.6/n² eV. E₃ = −13.6/9 ≈ −1.51 eV.

FAB=i(B) (Attractive) FAB=i(L).μ0i2π(L₂)(←)=μ0iiπ(←)FAB=i(L).2π(2L)μ0i(←)=πμ0ii(←) FBC(↑) and FAD(↓)⇒ cancels each other
FCD=i(B) (Repulsive) FCD=i(L)μ0i2π(3L2)(→)=μ0ii3π(→)FCD=i(L)2π(23L)μ0i(→)=3πμ0ii(→)⇒Fnet=μ0iiπ−μ0ii3π=2μ0ii3π⇒Fnet=πμ0ii−3πμ0ii=3π2μ0ii

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: Initial energy stored in capacitor 2μF Ui=122(V)2=V2Ui=212(V)2=V² Final voltage after switch 2 is ON Vf=C1V1C1+C₂=2V10=0.2VVf=C₁+C2C1V1=102V=0.2V Final energy in both the capacitors Uf=12(C₁+C₂)Vf2=1210(2V10)2=0.2V2Uf=21(C₁+C₂)Vf2=2110(102V)2=0.2V2So energy dissipated=V²−0.2V2×10^0=80%So energy dissipated=V2−0.2V2×10^0=80%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform rope of length L and mass m₁ hangs vertically from a rigid support. A block of mass m2 is attached to the free end of the rope. A transverse pulse of wavelength λ1 is produced at the lower end of the rope. The wavelength of the pulse when it reaches the top of the rope is λ2 . The ratio λ₂/λ1λ2/λ₁ is :
questions.explanation: T₁=m2gT1=m2g T₂=(m₁+m₂)gT2=(m₁+m₂)g Velocity ∝T λ∝T λ1λ2=T1T2λ1=T2T1 ⇒λ2λ1=m₁+m2⇒λ1λ2=m2m1+m₂

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A refrigerator works between 4∘C and 30∘C It is required to remove 600 calories of heat every second in order to keep the temperature of the refrigerated space constant. The power required is: (Take 1 cal =4.2 Joules)
questions.explanation: β=Q2W=T2T1−T2β=WQ2=T₁−T2 (Where Q2 is heat removed) ⇒600×4.2W=277303−277⇒W600×4.2=303−277⇒W=236.5Joule⇒Power=Wt=236.5Joule1sec=236.5wt.⇒Power=tW=1sec236.5Joule=236.5wt.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An air column, closed at one end and open at the other, resonates with a tuning fork when the smallest length of the column is 50 cm50 cm . The next larger length of the column resonating with the same tuning fork is :
questions.explanation: First minimum resonating length for closed organ pipe=λ4=50cmpipe=4λ=50cm Next larger length of air column =3λ4=150cm=43λ=150cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Consider the junction diode as ideal. The value of current flowing through AB is :
questions.explanation: Since diode is in forward bias i=ΔVR=4−(−6)1×10³=10¹⁰³=10−2Ai=RΔV=1×10³⁴−(−6)=10³¹⁰=10−2A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The charge flowing through a resistance R varies with time t as Q=at−bt2 , where a and b are positive constants. The total heat produced in R is:
questions.explanation: Q=at−bt2 i=a−2bt{fori=0⇒t=a2b}i=a−2bt{fori=0⇒t=2ba} From joule's law of heating dH=i2Rdt H=∫0a/2b(a−2bt)2RdtH=(a−2bt)3R−3×2b∣0a2b=a3R6bH=−3×2b(a−2bt)3R02ba=6ba3R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A black body is at a temperature of 5760 K. The energy of radiation emitted by the body at wavelength 250nm is U1 , at wavelength 500nm is U2 and that at 1000nm is U3 . Wien's constant, b=2.88×106nmK . Which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: Maximum amount of emitted radiation corresponding to λm=bTλm=Tb λm=2.88×106nmK5760K=500nmλm=5760K2.88×106nmK=500nm From the graph U₁<U₂>U3U1<U₂>U₃

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A npn transistor is connected in common emitter configuration in a given amplifier. A load resistance of 800Ω is connected in the collector circuit and the voltage drop across it is 0.8 V₀.8 V . If the current amplification factor is 0.96 and the input resistance of the circuit is 192Ω , the voltage gain and the power gain of the amplifier will respectively be:
questions.explanation: Given α=0.96 so,β=α₁−α=0.960.04⇒β=24so,β=1−αα=0.040.96⇒β=24 Voltage gain for common emitter configuration Av=β⋅RLRP=24×800192=100Av=β⋅RPRL=24×192800=100 Power gain for common emitter configuration Pv=βAv=24×10^0=2400 Voltage gain for common base configuration Av=α⋅RLRP=0.96×800192=4Av=α⋅RPRL=0.96×192800=4 Power gain for common base configuration Pv=Avα=4×0.96=3.84 *In the question it is asked about common emitter configuration but we got above answer for common base configuration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The intensity at the maximum in a Young's double slit experiment is I0 . Distance between two slits is d=5λ , where λλ is the wavelength of light used in the experiment. What will be the intensity in front of one of the slits on the screen placed at a distance D=10 dD=10 d ?
questions.explanation: Path difference =S2P−S1P=D²+d²−D=D(1+12d2D2)−D=D(1+21D2d2)−D=D[1+d22D2−1]=d22D=D[1+2D2d2−1]=2Dd2Δx=d22×10d=d20=5λ20=λ4Δx=2×10dd2=20d=205λ=4λΔϕ=2πλ⋅λ4=π2Δϕ=λ2π⋅4λ=2π So, intensity at the desired point is I=I0cos2ϕ2=I0cos2π4=I02I=I0cos22ϕ=I0cos24π=2I0

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A uniform circular disc of radius 50 cm50 cm at rest is free to turn about an axis which is perpendicular to its plane and passes through its centre. It is subjected to a torque which produces a constant angular acceleration of 2.0rads−2 . Its net acceleration in ms−2 at the end of 2.0 s 2.0 s is approximately :
questions.explanation: Particle at periphery will have both radial and tangential acceleration at=Rα=0.5×2=1m/s2ω=ω₀+αtω=0+2×2=4rad/secac=ω2R=(4)2×0.5=16×0.5=8m/s2atotal=ap2+ac2=12+82≈8m/s2 *In this question we have assumed the point to be located at periphery of the disc.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A disk and a sphere of same radius but different masses roll off on two inclined planes of the same altitude and length. Which one of the two objects gets to the bottom of the plane first ?

options.C: Both reach at the same time

questions.explanation: a=gsin⁡θ₁+K2R2a=1+R2K2gsinθ for disc;K2R2=12=0.5for disc;R2K2=21=0.5for sphere;K2R2=25=0.4for sphere;R2K2=52=0.4 a(sphere) >> a(disc) sphere reaches first

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The angle of incidence for a ray of light at a refracting surface of a prism is 45∘ . The angle of prism is 60∘ . If the ray suffers minimum deviation through the prism, the angle of minimum deviation and refractive index of the material of the prism respectively, are :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When an αα -particle of mass mm moving with velocity vv bombards on a heavy nucleus of charge Ze , its distance of closest approach from the nucleus depends on m as :
questions.explanation: At closest distance of approach, the kinetic energy of the particle will convert completely into electrostatic potential energy. ⇒12mv2=KQqd⇒d∝1m⇒21mv2=dKQq⇒d∝m₁

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass 10g moves along a circle of radius 6.4cm with a constant tangential acceleration. What is the magnitude of this acceleration if the kinetic energy of the particle becomes equal to 8×10−4J by the end of the second revolution after the beginning of the motion?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The molecules of a given mass of a gas have r.m.s. velocity of 200ms−1 at 27∘C and 1.0×105Nm−2 pressure. When the temperature and pressure of the gas are respectively, 127∘C and 0.05×105Nm−2 , the r.m.s. velocity of its molecules in ms−1 is :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long straight wire of radius a carries a steady current I. The current is uniformly distributed over its cross-section. The ratio of the magnetic fields B and B', at radial distances a22a and 2a respectively, from the axis of the wire is :
questions.explanation: For points inside the wire B=μ0Ir2πR2(r≤R)B=2πR2μ0Ir(r≤R) For points outside the wire B=μ0I2πr(r≥R)B=2πrμ0I(r≥R) according to the question BB′=μ0I(a/2)2πa2μ0I2π(2a)=1:1B′B=2π(2a)μ0I2πa2μ0I(a/2)=1:1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle moves so that its position vector is given by τ⃗=cos⁡ωtx^+sin⁡ωty^τ=cosωtx^+sinωty^ . Where ωω is a constant. Which of the following is true ?

options.A: Velocity and acceleration both are perpendicular to τ⃗τ .
options.B: Velocity and acceleration both are parallel to τ⃗τ
options.C: Velocity is perpendicular to τ⃗τ and acceleration is directed towards the origin
options.D: Velocity is perpendicular to τ⃗τ and acceleration is directed away from the origin

questions.explanation: τ⃗=cos⁡ωtx^+sin⁡ωty^τ=cosωtx^+sinωty^ υ⃗=−ωsin⁡ωtx^+ωcos⁡ωty^υ=−ωsinωtx^+ωcosωty^ a⃗=−ω2cos⁡ωtx^+ωsin⁡ωty^=−ω2τ⃗a=−ω2cosωtx^+ωsinωty^=−ω2τ τ⃗.υ⃗=0τ.υ=0 hence τ⃗⊥υ⃗τ⊥υ a⃗a is directed towards the origin.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is the minimum velocity with which a body of mass m must enter a vertical loop of radius R so that it can complete the loop ?
questions.explanation: When minimum speed of body is 5gR , then no matter from where it enters the loop, it will complete full vertical loop.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a metallic surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength λλ , the stopping potential is V. If the same surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength 2λ , the stopping potential is V44V . The threshold wavelength for the metallic surface is :
questions.explanation: eV=hcλ−hcλ0eV=λhc−λ0hc ...(i) eV/4=hc2λ−hcλ0eV/4=2λhc−λ0hc ...(ii) From equation (i) and (ii) ⇒4=1λ−1λ012λ−1λ0⇒4=2λ1−λ01 On solving λ0=3λ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A potentiometer wire is 100cm long and a constant potential difference is maintained across it. Two cells are connected in series first to support one another and then in opposite direction. The balance points are obtained at 50 cm50 cm and 10 cm10 cm from the positive end of the wire in the two cases. The ratio of emfs is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A astronomical telescope has objective and eyepiece of focal lengths 40 cm and 4 cm respectively. To view an object 200 cm away from the objective, the lenses must be separated by a distance :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two non-mixing liquids of densities ρρ and nρ (n > 1) are put in a container. The height of each liquid is h. A solid cylinder of length L and density d is put in this container. The cylinder floats with its axis vertical and length pL(p < 1) in the denser liquid. The density d is equal to :-
questions.explanation: L A d g = (pL) A (nρρ)g + (1 – p) L A ρρ g ⇒d=(1–p)ρ+pnρ=[1+(n–1)p]ρ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: To get output 1 for the following circuit, the correct choice for the input is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The ratio of escape velocity at earth (ve) to the escape velocity at a planet (vp) whose radius and mean density are twice as that of earth is :-
questions.explanation: Ve=2GMR=2GR⋅43πR3ρ∝RρVe=R2GM=R2G⋅34πR3ρ∝Rρ ∴∴ Ratio = 1 : 2√2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the magnitude of sum of two vectors is equal to the magnitude of difference of the two vectors, the angle between these vectors is :-
questions.explanation: ∣Aˉ+Bˉ∣=∣Aˉ−Bˉ∣=0=90∘.Aˉ+Bˉ=Aˉ−Bˉ=0=90∘.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Given the value of Rydberg constant is 107m−1 , the wave number of the last line of the Balmer series in hydrogen spectrum will be :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1kg begins to move under the action of a time dependent force Fˉ=(2ti^+3t2j^)N where i^ and j^ are unit vectors along xx and yy axis. What power will be developed by the force at the time tt ?
questions.explanation: Fˉ=2ti^+3t2j^ mdvˉdt=2ti^+3t2j^mdtdvˉ=2ti^+3t2j^ (m=1kg) ⇒∫0vˉdvˉ=∫0t(2ti^+3t2j^)dt⇒vˉ=t2i^+t3j^ Power =Fˉ.vˉ=(2t³+3t⁵)W

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor 20mH , a capacitor 50μF and a resistor 40Ω are connected in series across a source of emf V=10 sin 340t . The power loss in A.C. circuit is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the velocity of a particle is v=At+Bt2 , where A and B are constants, then the distance travelled by it between 1s and 2s is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long solenoid has 1000 turns. When a current of 4A flows through it, the magnetic flux linked with each turn of the solenoid is 4×10⁻³⁴×10⁻³ Wb. The self inductance of the solenoid is :
questions.explanation: Flux linked with each turn =4×10⁻³ Wb ∴Total flux linked=1000[4×10⁻³]Wb∴Total flux linked=1000[4×10⁻³]Wb ϕtotal=4⇒Li=4⇒L=1H

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 10 kg moving with speed of 3 m/s collides with another stationary body of mass 5 kg. As a result, the two bodies stick together. The KE of composite mass will be
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: 10(3) = 15V, V = 2 m/s. KE = ½(15)(2²) = 30 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Select the false statement about collisions

options.A: In elastic collision, KE is not conserved during the collision
options.B: The coefficient of restitution for a collision between two steel balls lies between 0 and 1
options.C: The momentum of a ball colliding elastically with the floor is conserved
options.D: In an oblique elastic collision between two identical bodies with one of them at rest initially, the final velocities are perpendicular

questions.explanation: Momentum of a ball colliding elastically with floor is NOT conserved as floor exerts external impulse on the system.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass m moving with a velocity u strikes a block of mass M at rest and gets embedded in the block. The loss of kinetic energy in the impact is
questions.explanation: ΔKE = ½ × m₁m₂/(m₁+m₂) × (u₁-u₂)² = mMu²/[2(m+M)]

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass m moving with velocity v strikes a suspended wooden block of mass M. If the block rises to height h, the initial velocity of the bullet will be
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: mv = (m+M)v₁, v₁ = √(2gh), so v = (M+m)/m × √(2gh)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is allowed to fall from a height of 10 m. If there is 40% loss of energy due to impact, then after one impact ball will go up by
questions.explanation: 60% energy retained. 0.6 × mg(10) = mgh, h = 6 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet weighing 10 g and moving with a velocity 300 m/s strikes a 5 kg block of ice and drops dead. The ice block is kept on smooth surface. The speed of the block after the collision is
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: (10/1000)(300) = (5 + 10/1000)v, v = 0.6 m/s = 60 cm/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m moving eastward with speed v collides with another particle of the same mass moving northwards with same speed v. The two particles coalesce on collision. The new particle of mass 2m will move with velocity
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation in x and y: resultant = v/√2 in North-East direction

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two perfectly elastic particles A and B of equal masses travelling along the line joing them with velocity 15 m/s and 10 m/s respectively, collide. Their velocities after the elastic collision will be (in m/s)
questions.explanation: In elastic collision between equal masses, velocities interchange: v₁ = 10 m/s, v₂ = 15 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two balls of equal mass undergo head on collision while each was moving with speed 6 m/s. If the coefficient of restitution is 1/3, the speed of each ball after impact will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball of mass M moving with speed v collides perfectly inelastically with another ball of mass m at rest. The magnitude of impulse imparted to the first ball is
questions.explanation: Impulse = change in momentum of first ball = Mv - M×[Mv/(M+m)] = Mmv/(M+m)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A force F = (3i + 4j) N acts on a particle moving in x-y plane. Starting from origin, the particle first goes along x-axis to the point (4,0) m and then parallel to y-axis to the point (4,3) m. The total work done by the force on the particle is
questions.explanation: Displacement = 4i + 3j. W = F·d = (3i+4j)·(4i+3j) = 12 + 12 = 24 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass 20 g leaves a rifle at initial speed 100 m/s and strikes a target at the same level with speed 50 m/s. The amount of work done by the resistance of air will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone with weight w is thrown vertically upward with initial speed v₀. If a constant force f due to air drag acts throughout its flight, the maximum height attained is
questions.explanation: By work-energy theorem: -fh - wh = -½mv₀², solving: h = v₀²/[2g(1 + f/w)]

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A knife of mass m is at a height x from a large wooden block. The knife is allowed to fall freely, strikes the block and comes to rest after penetrating distance y. The work done by the wooden block to stop the knife is
questions.explanation: By work-energy theorem: mg(x+y) + W_block = 0, so W_block = -mg(x+y)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A man is running on horizontal road with half the KE of a boy of half his mass. When man speeds up by 1 m/s, his KE becomes equal to KE of the boy. The original speed of the man is
questions.explanation: Setting up equations: ½(2m)V²/2 = ½(m)(v_boy)² and ½(2m)(V+1)² = ½(m)(v_boy)², solving gives V = √2 + 1 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m starts moving from origin along x-axis and its velocity varies with position x as v = k√x. The work done by force acting on it during first t seconds is
questions.explanation: From v = k√x: a = k²/2, x = ½at² = k²t²/4. W = F×x = m(k²/2)(k²t²/4) = mk⁴t²/8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle is moving in a circular path of radius r under the action of a force F. If at an instant velocity of particle is v, and speed of particle is increasing, then
questions.explanation: Net force has both centripetal and tangential components. Angle between F and v is between 0° and 90°, so F·v > 0

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The kinetic energy K of a particle moving along x-axis varies with its position x as shown in figure. The magnitude of force acting on particle at x = 9 m is
questions.explanation: F = dK/dx = slope of K-x curve at x = 9 m = 5 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 2 kg is released from the top of an inclined smooth surface. If spring constant is 100 N/m and block comes to rest after compressing the spring by 1 m, the distance travelled by block before it comes to rest is
questions.explanation: mgh sin45° = ½k(1)², solving h = 5 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If net force on a system is zero then

options.C: The acceleration of its constituent particle may be non-zero

questions.explanation: Due to internal forces, KE and acceleration of constituent particles may be non-zero even when net external force is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Internal forces acting within a system of particles can alter

options.A: The linear momentum as well as the kinetic energy of the system
options.B: The linear momentum of the system, but not the kinetic energy
options.C: The kinetic energy of the system, but not the linear momentum
options.D: Neither linear momentum nor kinetic energy

questions.explanation: Internal forces can change KE but not total linear momentum as F_ext = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the figure, a particle is released from position A on a smooth track. When the particle reaches B, then normal reaction on it by the track is
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation from A(height 3h) to B(height 2h): v² = 2gh. At B: mg + N = mv²/h = m(2gh)/h, N = mg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is projected with speed u at angle θ with horizontal from ground. The work done by gravity on it during its upward motion is
questions.explanation: Height = u²sin²θ/2g. W = -mg × u²sin²θ/2g = -mu²sin²θ/2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle is moving along a vertical circle of radius R. At P, what will be the velocity of particle (assume critical condition at C)?
questions.explanation: At critical condition at C: v_c = √(gR). Using energy conservation from P to C with 60° angle: v = √(2gR)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle attached to end of string of length l is released from horizontal position. When vertically below O, string contacts nail N at distance h below O. For particle to swing completely around the nail,
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: mgl = ½m(5g(l-h)), solving: h = 3l/5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If F = 2x² - 3x - 2, then select the correct statement

options.A: x = -1/2 is the position of stable equilibrium
options.B: x = 2 is the position of stable equilibrium
options.C: x = -1/2 is the position of unstable equilibrium
options.D: x = 2 is the position of neutral equilibrium

questions.explanation: Setting F=0: x = 2 or x = -1/2. At x = -1/2, d²V/dx² > 0 → stable equilibrium

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a conservative force does positive work on a body, then the

options.C: Total mechanical energy of body increases
options.D: Total mechanical energy of body decreases

questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx, so when F does positive work, dU is negative → PE decreases

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The variation of force F acting on a body moving along x-axis varies with its position x. The body is in stable equilibrium state at
questions.explanation: Stable equilibrium is where F=0 and dF/dx < 0 (i.e., -d²U/dx² < 0), which corresponds to Q

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle in one dimensional potential field has U(x) = a/x² - b/x³. The position of equilibrium corresponds to x =
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx = 0 at equilibrium: -2a/x³ + 3b/x⁴ = 0, solving x = 3b/2a

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The force required to row a boat at constant velocity is proportional to square of its speed. If a speed of v km/h requires 4 kW, how much power does a speed of 2v km/h require?
questions.explanation: F ∝ v², P = Fv ∝ v³. P₁/P₂ = v³/(2v)³ = 1/8, so P₂ = 32 kW

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m is projected from ground with speed u at angle θ with horizontal. The power delivered by gravity at half of maximum height from ground is
questions.explanation: At half H_max, v_y = u sinθ/√2. P = -mg × v_y = -mgu sinθ/√2 (options 2 and 3 are equivalent)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m moves in circular path of radius r under constant power p. The angular acceleration of particle at time t is proportional to
questions.explanation: Pt = ½m(rω)², so ω² ∝ t. Using α = ω/t: α ∝ 1/√t

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rate of doing work by force acting on a particle moving along x-axis depends on position x and equals 2x. The velocity of particle is given by
questions.explanation: P = F·v = m(v dv/dx)v = 2x, so mv² dv = 2x dx, mv³/3 = x², v = (3x²/m)^(1/3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small ball of mass m moving with speed v undergoes elastic head on collision with a stationary bob of identical mass of a simple pendulum of length L. The maximum height h to which the bob rises after collision is
questions.explanation: In elastic collision of equal masses, full velocity transfers. ½mv² = mgh → h = v²/2g

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two balls of masses m each moving at right angles with velocities 6 m/s and 8 m/s respectively. If collision is perfectly inelastic, the velocity of combined mass is
questions.explanation: Resultant momentum = m√(6²+8²) = 10m. v = 10m/2m = 5 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A sphere of mass m moving with constant velocity u hits another stationary sphere of same mass. If e is coefficient of restitution, ratio of velocities v₂/v₁ after collision is
questions.explanation: v₁ = u/2(1+e), v₂ = u/2(1-e), ratio v₂/v₁ = (1-e)/(1+e)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A neutron travelling with a velocity collides elastically head on with a nucleus of mass number A at rest. The fraction of total energy retained by neutron is
questions.explanation: v₁ = v(A-1)/(A+1). Fraction = v₁²/v² = ((A-1)/(A+1))²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small ball hits obliquely a smooth horizontal surface with speed u whose x and y components are indicated. If coefficient of restitution is 1/2, its x and y components vx and vy just after collision are respectively
questions.explanation: vy = e × uy = ½ × 4 = 2 m/s; vx = ux = 2 m/s (no friction on smooth surface)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object of mass M₁ moving horizontally with speed u collides elastically with another object of mass M₂ at rest. Select correct statement

options.A: The momentum of system is conserved only in direction PQ
options.B: Momentum of M₁ is conserved in direction perpendicular to SR
options.C: Momentum of M₂ will change in direction normal to CR

questions.explanation: In oblique collision, momentum of M₁ is conserved perpendicular to the line of impact (SR)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball of mass m moving with speed u collides with a smooth horizontal surface at angle θ. The magnitude of impulse imparted to surface by ball [coefficient of restitution e]
questions.explanation: Normal component: u_y = -u sinθ, v_y = +eu sinθ. Impulse = m(v_y - u_y) = mu(1+e)sinθ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m falls from height h. If e is coefficient of restitution, the distance travelled before it comes to rest is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The PE of a 2 kg particle is given by V(x) = (x³/3 - x²/2) J. The total mechanical energy is 4 J. Maximum speed (in m/s) is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two particles of masses m₁, m₂ move with initial velocities u₁ and u₂. On collision, one particle gets excited absorbing energy ε. If final velocities are v₁ and v₂ then we must have
questions.explanation: Initial KE minus absorbed energy ε equals final KE: KE_i - ε = KE_f

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two similar springs P and Q have spring constants KP > KQ. They are stretched first by same amount (case a), then by same force (case b). Work done WP and WQ are related as
questions.explanation: Case a: W = ½kx², KP > KQ → WP > WQ. Case b: W = F²/2k, KP > KQ → WQ > WP

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is driven by a machine delivering constant power k watts. If particle starts from rest, force on particle at time t is
questions.explanation: P = F·v = const k. v = √(2kt/m). F = ma = m dv/dt = √(mk/2) t^(-1/2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 10 kg moving in x direction with constant speed 10 m/s is subjected to retarding force F = 0.1x J/m during travel from x=20 m to x=30 m. Its final KE will be
questions.explanation: Initial KE = ½(10)(10²) = 500 J. W = -∫₂₀³⁰ 0.1x dx = -0.1[x²/2]₂₀³⁰ = -25 J. Final KE = 475 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 4m lying at rest explodes into three pieces. Two pieces each of mass m move perpendicular to each other with equal speeds v. Total KE generated is
questions.explanation: Third piece has mass 2m and velocity v/√2 (by momentum conservation). Total KE = ½mv² + ½mv² + ½(2m)(v/√2)² = 3mv²/2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform force (3i + j) N acts on particle of mass 2 kg displaced from position (2i + k) m to position (4i + 3j - k) m. Work done is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Potential energy of a particle is U = A/r² - B/r. For stable equilibrium, the distance is
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dr = 0: 2A/r³ - B/r² = 0, r = 2A/B

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two spheres A and B of masses m₁ and m₂ collide. A is at rest initially and B moving with velocity v along x-axis. After collision B has velocity v/2 perpendicular to original direction. Mass A moves after collision in the direction
questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: m₂v i = -m₂(v/2)j + m₁v_A. θ = tan⁻¹(v/2 / v) = tan⁻¹(1/2) to x-axis

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone dropped from height h hits the ground with momentum P. If same stone is dropped from height 100% more, momentum when it hits the ground will change by

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car starts from rest with constant power P₀. The instaneous velocity is proportional to
questions.explanation: W = P₀t = ½mv², so v² ∝ t, v ∝ t^(1/2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The potential energy of a system increases if work is done

options.A: Upon the system by a conservative force
options.B: Upon the system by a non-conservative force
options.C: By the system against a conservative force
options.D: By the system against a non-conservative force

questions.explanation: dU = -∫F_c·dx. When system does work against conservative force, U increases

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Force F on a particle varies with distance d as shown. Work done during displacement of 12 m is
questions.explanation: W = area under F-x curve = ½×5×2 + 4×2 = 5 + 8 = 13 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water through a hose pipe. Water leaves with velocity 2 m/s. Mass per unit length of water in pipe is 100 kg/m. Power of the engine is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass M starting from rest undergoes uniform acceleration. If speed acquired in time T is V, the power delivered to particle is
questions.explanation: W = ½MV². P = W/T = MV²/2T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water continuously. Water leaves hose with velocity v and m is mass per unit length of water jet. Rate at which KE is imparted to water is
questions.explanation: Rate of KE = ½(dm/dt)v² = ½(mv)v² = ½mv³

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1 kg is thrown upwards with velocity 20 m/s. It comes to rest after attaing height 18 m. Energy lost due to air friction is (g=10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: W_friction + W_gravity = ΔKE = 0 - ½(1)(20²). W_f - mg(18) = -200. W_f = 200 - 180 = 20 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass M is attached to lower end of vertical spring of force constant k. Released from rest with spring unstretched. Maximum extension in spring will be
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: Mgx = ½kx², x_max = 2Mg/k

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Water falls from height 60 m at rate 15 kg/s to operate turbine. Losses due to friction are 10%. Power generated by turbine (g=10 m/s²) is
questions.explanation: Power input = (dm/dt)gh = 15×60×10 = 9000 W. After 10% loss = 9000×0.9 = 8100 W = 8.1 kW

questions.subject: Physics

options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two identical capacitors and C2 of equal capacitance are connected as shown in the circuit. Terminals a and b of the key k are connected to charge capacitor C1 using battery of emf V volt. Now disconnecting a and b the terminals b and c are connected. Due to this, what will be the percentage loss of energy?
questions.explanation: Ui=21CV2 On switching key at point c cq0−q=cq 2q=q0 q=(2q0) Ut=21(2q0)2×c1+21(2q0)2×c1 Uf=4Cq02 Uf=41CV2 loss=(Ui−Uf)×100 =21cv2(21−41)cv2×10^0 =50%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two bullets are fired horizontally and simultaneously towards each other from roof tops of two buildings 100 m apart and of same height of 200 m, with the same velocity of 25 m/s. When and where will the two bullets collide? (g=10 m/s2)

options.B: After 2 s at a height of 180 m
options.C: After 2 s at a height of 20 m
options.D: After 4 s at a height of 120 m

questions.explanation: Let bullets collide at time t x1+x²=100 m 25t+25t=100 t=2 s y=21gt2=21×10×22 =20m h=200−20=180 m Hence bullets will collide after 2 s at height 180 m above the ground.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The stress-strain curves are drawn for two different materials X and Y. It is observed that the ultimate strength point and the fracture point are close to each other for material X but are far apart for material Y. We can say that materials X and Y are likely to be (respectively),
questions.explanation: As given that fracture point and ultimate strength point is close for material X, hence X is brittle in nature and both points are far apart for material Y hence it is ductile. i.e X is brittle and Y is ductile in nature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m is kept on a rough horizontal surface (coefficient of friction =μ). A horizontal force is applied on the body, but it does not move. The resultant of normal reaction and the frictional force acting on the object is given by F, where F is,
questions.explanation: Since body does not move hence it is in equilibrium. fr= frictional force which is less than or equal to limiting friction. Now N=mg Hence F=N+fr ∣F∣≤(mg)2+(μmg)2 ∣F∣≤mg1+μ2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass 5m at rest suddenly breaks on its own into three fragments. Two fragments of mass m each move along mutually perpendicular direction with speed v each. The energy released during the process is,
questions.explanation: From conservation of linear momentum. 0=mvj^+mvi^+3mv1 v1=−3v(i^+j^) v1=32v KEi=0 KEf=21mv2+21(3m)(32)2v2 =mv2+3mv2=34mv2 ΔKE=KEf−KEi=34mv2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object of mass 500 g, initially at rest, is acted upon by a variable force whose X-component varies with X in the manner shown. The velocities of the object at the points x=8 m and X = 12 m, would have the respective values of (nearly) Shutterstock
questions.explanation: From work-energy theorem AK = work = area under F - x graph From x=0 to x=8m 21mv2=100+30 v²=520 v=520=23 m/s From x=0 to x=12m 21mv2=100+30−47.5+20 v=410 v=20.6 m/s Hence appropriate option is 23 m/s and 20.6 m/s.

questions.explanation: Since v=1+R2K22gh v²=1+212gh R2K2=21 for solid cylinder 2gh=42×23 h=1012=1.2m Now x=sin 30∘h=211.2=2.4m x=2.4m

questions.explanation: Sol. Vescape = 11200m / s Say at temperature T it attains V escape So1√(3kBT/mO2) = 11200m / s On solving, T = 8.360×104K

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A set of 'n' equal resistors, of value 'R' each, are connected in series to a battery of emf 'E' and internal resistance 'R'. The current drawn is 1. Now, the 'n' resistors are connected in parallel to the same battery. Then the current drawn from battery becomes 10 1. The value of 'n' is
questions.explanation: Sol. 1 = E/(nR + R) 101 = E/(R/n + R) Dividing (ii) by (i), 10 = ((n + 1)R)/((1/n + 1)R) After solving the equation, n = 10
