questions.question: Unpolarised light is incident from air on a plane surface of a material of refractive index 'μ'. At a particular angle of incidence 'i', it is found that the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular to each other. Which of the following options is correct for this situation?

options.B: Reflected light is polarised with its electric vector perpendicular to the plane of incidence
options.C: Reflected light is polarised with its electric vector parallel to the plane of incidence

questions.explanation: Sol. When reflected light rays and refracted rays are perpendicular, reflected light is polarised with electric field vector perpendicular to the plane of incidence. Also, tan i = μ (Brewster angle)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Young's double slit experiment the separation d between the slits is 2mm , the wavelength λ of the light used is 5896 Å and distance D between the screen and slits is 100cm . It is found that the angular width of the fringes is 0.20° . To increase the fringe angular width to 0.21° (with same λ and D) the separation between the slits needs to be changed to
questions.explanation: Sol. Angular width = λ/d 0.20° = λ/2 mm …(i) 0.21° = λ/d …(ii) Dividing we get, 0.20/0.21 = d/2 mm ∴ d = 1.9 mm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An astronomical refracting telescope will have large angular magnification and high angular resolution, when it has an objective lens of

options.A: Large focal length and large diameter
options.B: Large focal length and small diameter
options.C: Small focal length and large diameter
options.D: Small focal length and small diameter

questions.explanation: Sol. For telescope, angular magnification = f0/fE So, focal length of objective lens should be large. Angular resolution = D/1.22λ should be large. So, objective should have large focal length (f0) and large diameter D.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The ratio of kinetic energy to the total energy of an electron in a Bohr orbit of the hydrogen atom, is
questions.explanation: Sol. KE = –(total energy) So, Kinetic energy : total energy = 1 : –1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron of mass m with an initial velocity V→ = V0 iˆ (V0 > 0) enters an electric field E→ = –E0 iˆ (E0 = constant > 0) at t = 0. If λ0 is its de-Broglie wavelength initially, then its de-Broglie wavelength at time t is
questions.explanation: Sol. Initial de-Broglie wavelength λ0 = h/mV0 Acceleration of electron = eE0/m Velocity after time ‘t’ V = V0 + (eE0/m)t So, λ = h/mV = h/m(V0 + (eE0/m)t) = h/mV0(1 + (eE0/mV0)t) = λ0/(1 + (eE0/mV0)t)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For a radioactive material, half-life is 10 minutes. If initially there are 600 number of nuclei, the time taken (in minutes) for the disintegration of 450 nuclei is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When the light of frequency 2v0 (where v0 is threshold frequency), is incident on a metal plate, the maximum velocity of electrons emitted is v1 . When the frequency of the incident radiation is increased to 5v0 , the maximum velocity of electrons emitted from the same plate is v² . The ratio of v1 to v² is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the circuit shown in the figure, the input voltage V1 is 20V , VBE = 0 and VCE = 0 . The values of IB, IC and β are given by

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a p-n junction diode, change in temperature due to heating

options.A: Does not affect resistance of p-n junction
options.D: Affects the overall V - I characteristics of p-n junction

questions.explanation: Sol. Due to heating, number of electron-hole pairs will increase, so overall resistance of diode will change. Due to which forward biasing and reversed biasing both are changed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An em wave is propagating in a medium with a velocity ν→ = νi→ . The instaneous oscillating electric field of this em wave is along +y axis. Then the direction of oscillating magnetic field of the em wave will be along
questions.explanation: Sol. E→ × B→ = V→ (E→) × (B→) = Vi→ So, B→ = Bk→ Direction of propagation is along +z direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The refractive index of the material of a prism is √2 and the angle of the prism is 30° . One of the two refracting surfaces of the prism is made a mirror inwards, by silver coating. A beam of monochromatic light entering the prism from the other face will retrace its path (after reflection from the silvered surface) if its angle of incidence on the prism is
questions.explanation: Sol. For retracing its path, light ray should be normally incident on silvered face. Applying Snell's law at M, sin i/ sin 30° = √2/1 ⇒ sin i = √2 × 1/2 sin i = 1/√2 i.e. i = 45°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object is placed at a distance of 40cm from a concave mirror of focal length 15cm. If the object is displaced through a distance of 20cm towards the mirror, the displacement of the image will be
questions.explanation: Sol. 1/f = 1/v1 + 1/u1 1/-15 = 1/v1 + 1/(-40) ⇒ 1/v1 = -1/15 + 1/40 v1 = -24cm When object is displaced by 20cm towards mirror. Now, u2 = -20 1/f = 1/v² + 1/u2 1/-15 = 1/v² + 1/(-20) 1/v² = 1/20 - 1/15 v² = -60cm So, image shifts away from mirror by = 60 - 24 = 36cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic potential energy stored in a certain inductor is 25mJ , when the current in the inductor is 60mA . This inductor is of inductance

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron falls from rest through a vertical distance h in a uniform and vertically upward directed electric field E. The direction of electric field is now reversed, keeping its magnitude the same. A proton is allowed to fall from rest in it through the same vertical distance h. The time of fall of the electron, in comparison to the time of fall of the proton is
questions.explanation: Sol. h = 1/2 (eE/m) t² ∴ t = √(2hm/eE) ∴ t ∝ √m as 'e' is same for electron and proton. Electron has smaller mass so it will take smaller time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electrostatic force between the metal plates of an isolated parallel plate capacitor C having a charge Q and area A, is

options.A: Proportional to the square root of the distance between the plates
options.B: Linearly proportional to the distance between the plates
options.C: Independent of the distance between the plates
options.D: Inversely proportional to the distance between the plates

questions.explanation: Sol. For isolated capacitor Q = Constant Fplate = Q2/(2Aε0) F is Independent of the distance between plates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A pendulum is hung from the roof of a sufficiently high building and is moving freely to and fro like a simple harmonic oscillator. The acceleration of the bob of the pendulum is 20m/s2 at a distance of 5m from the mean position. The time period of oscillation is
questions.explanation: Sol. |a| = ω2y ⇒ 20 = ω2(5) ⇒ ω = 2rad/s T = 2π/ω = 2π/2 = πs

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A metallic rod of mass per unit length 0.5 kg m–1 is lying horizontally on a smooth inclined plane which makes an angle of 30° with the horizontal. The rod is not allowed to slide down by flowing a current through it when a magnetic field of induction 0.25 T is acting on it in the vertical direction. The current flowing in the rod to keep it stationary is
questions.explanation: Sol. For equilibrium, mg sin30° = I/B cos30° I = (mg/l) (tan30°/B) I = (0.5 × 9.8)/(0.25 × √3) = 11.32 A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A thin diamagnetic rod is placed vertically between the poles of an electromagnet. When the current in the electromagnet is switched on, then the diamagnetic rod is pushed up, out of the horizontal magnetic field. Hence the rod gains gravitational potential energy. The work required to do this comes from

options.A: The lattice structure of the material of the rod
options.D: The induced electric field due to the changing magnetic field

questions.explanation: Sol. Energy of current source will be converted into potential energy of the rod.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor 20 mH, a capacitor 100 μF and a resistor 50 Ω are connected in series across a source of emf, V = 10 sin 314 t. The power loss in the circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Current sensitivity of a moving coil galvanometer is 5 div/mA and its voltage sensitivity (angular deflection per unit voltage applied) is 20 div/V. The resistance of the galvanometer is
questions.explanation: Sol. Current sensitivity = NBA/C = IS Voltage sensitivity = NBA/CRG = VS So, resistance of galvanometer = IS/VS = (5 × 10⁻3)/(20) = 5000/20 = 250 Ω

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body initially at rest and sliding along a frictionless track from a height h (as shown in the figure) just completes a vertical circle of diameter AB = D. The height h is equal to
questions.explanation: Sol. As track is frictionless, so total mechanical energy will remain constant T.M.EI = T.M.EF 0 + mgh = 1/2 mvL2 + 0 h = vL2/2g For completing the vertical circle, vL ≥ √5gR h = 5gR/2g = 5/2 R = 5/4 D

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Three objects, A : (a solid sphere), B : (a thin circular disk) and C : (a circular ring), each have the same mass M and radius R. They all spin with the same angular speed ω about their own symmetry axes. The amounts of work (W) required to bring them to rest, would satisfy the relation
questions.explanation: Sol. Work done required to bring them rest ΔW = ΔKE ΔW = 1/2 Iω2 ΔW ∝ I for same ω WA : WB : WC = 2/5 MR² : 1/2 MR² : MR² = 2/5 : 1/2 : 1 = 4 : 5 : 10 ⇒ WC > WB > WA

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A moving block having mass m, collides with another stationary block having mass 4m. The lighter block comes to rest after collision. When the initial velocity of the lighter block is v, then the value of coefficient of restitution (e) will be
questions.explanation: Sol. According to law of conservation of linear momentum, mv + 4m × 0 = 4mv' + 0 v' = v/4 e = (Relative velocity of separation)/(Relative velocity of approach) = (v'/4)/(v) e = 1/4 = 0.25

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?

options.A: Frictional force opposes the relative motion.
options.B: Limiting value of static friction is directly proportional to normal reaction.
options.C: Rolling friction is smaller than sliding friction.
options.D: Coefficient of sliding friction has dimensions of length.

questions.explanation: Sol. Coefficient of sliding friction has no dimension. f = μsN ⇒ μs = f/N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A toy car with charge q moves on a frictionless horizontal plane surface under the influence of a uniform electric field E→ Due to the force qE→ its velocity increases from 0 to 6m/s in one second duration. At that instant the direction of the field is reversed. The car continues to move for two more seconds under the influence of this field. The average velocity and the average speed of the toy car between 0 to 3 seconds are respectively
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A student measured the diameter of a small steel ball using a screw gauge of least count 0.001 cm. The main scale reading is 5 mm and zero of circular scale division coincides with 25 divisions above the reference level. If screw gauge has a zero error of -0.004cm , the correct diameter of the ball is
questions.explanation: Sol. Diameter of the ball = MSR + CSR × (Least count) - Zero error = 0.5cm + 25 × 0.001 - (-0.004) = 0.5 + 0.025 + 0.004 = 0.529cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A solid sphere is rotating freely about its symmetry axis in free space. The radius of the sphere is increased keeping its mass same. Which of the following physical quanties would remain constant for the sphere?
questions.explanation: Sol. τex = 0 So, dL/dt = 0 i.e. L = constant So angular momentum remains constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The kinetic energies of a planet in an elliptical orbit about the Sun, at positions A, B and C are KA KB and KC respectively. AC is the major axis and SB is perpendicular to AC at the position of the Sun S as shown in the figure. Then
questions.explanation: Sol. Point A is perihelion and C is aphelion. So, VA > VB > VC So, KA > KB > KC

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the mass of the Sun were ten times smaller and the universal gravitational constant were ten times larger in magnitude, which of the following is not correct?

options.A: Time period of a simple pendulum on the Earth would decrease
options.B: Walking on the ground would become more difficult
options.D: 'g' on the Earth will not change

questions.explanation: Sol. If Universal Gravitational constant becomes ten times, then G' = 10 G So, acceleration due to gravity increases. i.e. (4) is wrong option.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A solid sphere is in rolling motion. In rolling motion a body posses translational kinetic energy (KL) as well as rotational kinetic energy (Kr) simultaneously. The ratio KL : (KL + Kr) for the sphere is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small sphere of radius 'r' falls from rest in a viscous liquid. As a result, heat is produced due to viscous force. The rate of production of heat when the sphere attains its terminal velocity, is proportional to
questions.explanation: Sol. Power = 6πηrVT·VT = 6πηrVT2 VT ∝ r² ⇒ Power ∝ r5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The power radiated by a black body is P and it radiates maximum energy at wavelength, λ0. If the temperature of the black body is now changed so that it radiates maximum energy at wavelength 3/4 λ0 , the power radiated by it becomes nP. The value of n is
questions.explanation: Sol. We know, λmax T = constant (Wien's law) So, λmax1 T1 = λmax2 T2 ⇒ λ0 T = 3λ0/4 T' ⇒ T' = 4/3 T So, P2/P1 = (T'/T)4 = (4/3)4 = 256/81

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two wires are made of the same material and have the same volume. The first wire has cross-sectional area A and the second wire has cross-sectional area 3A. If the length of the first wire is increased by Δl on applying a force F, how much force is need to stretch the second wire by the same amount?
questions.explanation: Sol. For wire 1, Δl = (F/AY)3l ...(i) For wire 2, Stress = F'/3A = Y × Strain = Y × (Δl/l) ⇒ Δl = (F'/3AY) l ...(ii) From equation (i) & (ii), (F/AY)3l = (F'/3AY)l ⇒ F' = 9F

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A sample of 0.1g of water at 100°C and normal pressure (1.013×105Nm-2) requires 54 cal of heat energy to convert to steam at 100°C. If the volume of the steam produced is 167.1 cc, the change in internal energy of the sample, is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The correct order of N-compounds in its decreasing order of oxidation states is
questions.explanation: Sol. HNO₃, NO, N₂, NH4Cl Hence, the correct option is (3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following elements is unable to form MF63- ion?
questions.explanation: Sol. : 'B' has no vacant d- orbitals in its valence shell, so it can't extend its covalency beyond 4. i.e. 'B' cannot form the ion like MF63(-) i.e. BF63(-). Hence, the correct option is (1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform rope of length L and mass m1 hangs vertically from a rigid support. A block of mass m2 is attached to the free end of the rope. A transverse pulse of wavelength λ1 is produced at the lower end of the rope. The wavelength of the pulse when it reaches the top of the rope is λ2 . The ratio λ2/λ1 is :
questions.explanation: T1=m2g T2=(m1+m²)g Velocity ∝T λ∝T λ1λ2=T1T2λ1=T2T1 ⇒λ2λ1=m1+m2⇒λ1λ2=m2m1+m²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Coefficient of linear expansion of brass and steel rods are α1 and α2 . Lengths of brass and steel rods are ℓ1 and ℓ2 respectively. If (ℓ2−ℓ1) is maintained same at all temperatures, which one of the following relations holds good?
questions.explanation: Change in length for both rods should be same

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A gas is compressed isothermally to half its initial volume. The same gas is compressed separately through an adiabatic process until its volume is again reduced to half. Then :-

options.A: Compressing the gas isothermally will require more work to be done.
options.B: Compressing the gas through adiabatic process will require more work to be done.
options.C: Compressing the gas isothermally or adiabatically will require the same amount of work.
options.D: Which of the case (whether compression through isothermal or through adiabatic process) requires more work will depend upon the atomicity of the gas.

questions.explanation: Wext= negative of area with volume- axis W(adiabatic) >> W(isothermal)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A piece of ice falls from a height h so that it melts completely. Only one-quarter of the heat produced is absorbed by the ice and all energy of ice gets converted into heat during its fall. The value of h is : [Latent heat of ice is 3.4 × 10^5 J/kg and g = 10 N/kg]
questions.explanation: mgh4=mL4mgh=mL

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small signal voltage V(t)=V0 sin ωt is applied across an ideal capacitor C :

options.A: Current I(t), lags voltage V(t) by 90∘
options.B: Over a full cycle the capacitor C does not consume any energy from the voltage source.
options.C: Current I(t) is in phase with voltage V(t)
options.D: Current I(t) leads voltage V(t) by 180∘

questions.explanation: Power =Vrms⋅Irmscos⁡ϕ=Vrms⋅Irmscosϕ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car is negotiating a curved road of radius R. The road is banked at an angle θθ . the coefficient of friction between the tyres of the car and the road is μs . The maximum safe velocity on this road is :
questions.explanation: v2Rg(μs+tan⁡θ1−μstan⁡θ)Rgv2(1−μstanθμs+tanθ)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Wavelength of a 1 keV photon is 1.24×10−9 m1.24×10−9 m. What is the frequency of 1 MeV photon?
questions.explanation: The energy of a photon is inversely proportional to its wavelength. By comparing the given ratio of energies (1 keV to 1 MeV) and using the relation f=c/λ, the frequency is calculated to be 2.4×1020Hz.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Ultraviolet radiations of 6.2eV fall on an aluminium surface (work function 4.2eV ). The kinetic energy (in joule) of the fastest electron emitted is approximately
questions.explanation: The maximum kinetic energy is the incident energy minus the work function (6.2 eV - 4.2 eV = 2 eV). Converting 2 eV to joules by multiplying by 1.6×10−19 gives 3.2×10−19 J3.2×10−19 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: The work function of a metallic surface is 5.01eV. The photo electrons are emitted when light of wavelength 2000A˚ falls on it. The potential difference applied to stop the fastest photo electrons is [h=4.14×10−15eVs]
questions.explanation: The stopping potential is the maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons in eV, which is calculated as hcλ−w0λhc−w0. Solving this with the given values yields 1.2 eV, so the stopping potential is 1.2 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: A radio transmitter operates at a frequency of 880kHz and a power of 10kW. The number of photons emitted per second is
questions.explanation: The number of photons emitted per second is the total power divided by the energy per photon (hf). Calculating 10×103h×880×10^310×10^3 gives approximately 1.72×10^31.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Assuming photoemission to take place, the factor by which the maximum velocity of the emitted photo electrons changes when the wavelength of the incident radiation is increased four times, is (assuming work function to be negligible in comparison to hc/λ )
questions.explanation: The kinetic energy is hcλλhc. Since v²∝1λv2∝λ1, if the wavelength is increased four times, the velocity becomes half.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two waves having intensities in the ratio of 9 : 1 produce interference. The ratio of maximum to minimum intensity is equal to
questions.explanation: The ratio of maximum to minimum intensity is given by (I1+I₂)2(I1−I₂)2(I1+I₂)2. With intensity ratio 9:1, the amplitude ratio is 3:1, resulting in a maximum to minimum intensity ratio of 4:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the light is polarised by reflection, then the angle between reflected and refracted light is
questions.explanation: According to Brewster's law, when light is incident at the polarizing angle, the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular to each other, making an angle of 90° or π/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The fringe width in Young's double-slit experiment can be increased if we decrease

options.B: Distance between the source and the screen

questions.explanation: Fringe width (ββ) is given by λDddλD. It is inversely proportional to the slit separation dd, so decreasing dd increases the fringe width.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In case of Young's experiment

options.A: There are two virtual sources of light from same monochromatic source of light
options.B: Both the slits get light from a single monochromatic source of light
options.C: Two separate monochromatic sources of light of same wavelength are used

questions.explanation: For interference fringes to be observed, the sources must be coherent. This is achieved in Young's experiment by both slits receiving light from the same single monochromatic source.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If light from a galaxy observed on the earth's surface has a red shift, then

options.A: Galaxy is stationary w.r.t. the earth
options.C: Galaxy is receding from the earth

questions.explanation: A red shift means the observed wavelength is longer (shifted towards the red end of the spectrum) than the emitted wavelength. This occurs when the source of light is moving away from the observer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Digital movie projectors work on the principle of

options.D: Total internal reflection from optical fibres

questions.explanation: Digital movie projectors use Digital Light Processing (DLP) technology, which relies on an array of tiny mirrors (micromirrors) to reflect light and create the image.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A convex mirror is used to form an image of a real object. Then mark the wrong statement

options.A: The image lies between the pole and focus
options.B: The image is diminished in size

questions.explanation: A convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image for a real object. It cannot form a real image of a real object.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a light ray from a rarer medium is refracted into a denser medium, its
questions.explanation: By definition, light slows down in a denser medium. Since frequency remains constant, a decrease in velocity (v=fλ) also leads to a decrease in wavelength.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Numerically 1 gauss = x tesla, then x is
questions.explanation: 1 gauss = 10⁻4 T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron having a charge e moves with a velocity v in positive x direction. A magnetic field acts on it in positive y direction. The force on the electron acts in (where outward direction is taken as positive z-axis).
questions.explanation: F = -e (v × B) So using right hand thumb rule, F will be in –z direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a proton enters perpendicularly a magnetic field with velocity v, then time period of revolution is T. If proton enters with velocity 2v, then time period will be
questions.explanation: T = 2πm/qB, independent of v.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton, a deutron and an α-particle accelerated through the same potential difference enter a region of uniform magnetic field, moving at right angles to it. What is the ratio of their kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: K.E. = qV, V is same for all. K.E. ∝ q. So K.E.p : K.E.d : K.E.α = 1 : 1 : 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m carrying charge q is accelerated by a potential difference V. It enters perpendicularly in a region of uniform magnetic field B and executes circular arc of radius R, then m/q equals
questions.explanation: r = mv/qB = √(2mqV)/qB r2 = (2mqV)/(q2B2) => m/q = B2r2/2V

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The net charge in a current carrying wire is zero still magnetic field exerts a force on it, because a magnetic field exerts force on
questions.explanation: F = q (v × B). Moving charges are only electrons and magnetic field exert force on moving charges only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a charged particle enters perpendicularly in the uniform magnetic field then

options.A: Energy remains constant but momentum changes
options.B: Energy and momentum both remains constant
options.C: Momentum remains constant but energy changes
options.D: Neither energy nor momentum remains constant

questions.explanation: Work done by magnetic force will always be zero as F ⟂ v, so using work energy theorem, W = 0, so K = constant. P changes as direction of velocity changes.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The motion of a charged particle can be used to distinguish between a magnetic field and electric field in a certain region by firing the charge
questions.explanation: To distinguish between E and B fields, one can observe the path when fired from opposite directions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The correct expression for Lorentz force is
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charged particle moves in a gravity free space without change in velocity. Which of the following is not possible in that space?
questions.explanation: If E ≠ 0 and B = 0, E will definitely deflect / increase / decrease velocity of charge. If both E and B are non zero, they may balance each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire is bent in the form of an equilateral triangle of side 100 cm and carries a current of 2 A. It is placed in a magnetic field of induction 2.0 T directed perpendicular into the plane of paper. The direction and magnitude of magnetic force acting on each side of the triangle will be

options.A: 2 N, normal to the side towards centre of the triangle
options.B: 2 N, normal to the side away from the centre of the triangle
options.C: 4 N, normal to the side towards centre of the triangle
options.D: 4 N, normal to the side away from the centre of the triangle

questions.explanation: F = i(l × B) F = 2(1)(2) = 4 N, normal to side towards centre of triangle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The oscillator frequency of a cyclotron is 10 MHz. What should be the operating magnetic field for accelerating proton?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A negative charge is coming towards the observer. The direction of the magnetic field produced by it will be (as seen by observer)

options.C: In the direction of motion of charge
options.D: In the direction opposite to the motion of charge

questions.explanation: B will be clockwise as seen by observer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When equal current is passed through two coils, equal magnetic field is produced at their centres. If the ratio of number of turns in the coils is 8 : 15, then the ratio of their radii will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The radius of a circular current carrying coil is R. At what distance from the centre of the coil on its axis, the intensity of magnetic field will be 1/2√2 times that at the centre?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The figure below shows a current loop having two circular arcs joined by two radial lines. The magnetic field at O is
questions.explanation: B = B(arc1) - B(arc2) = (μ0iθ/4πa) - (μ0iθ/4πb) = μ0iθ/4π (1/a - 1/b) = μ0iθ/4π (b-a)/ab

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following graphs shows the variation of magnetic induction B with distance r from a long wire carrying a current?
questions.explanation: B = μ0i/2πr ⇒ B ∝ 1/r

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two straight long conductors AOB and COD are perpendicular to each other and carry currents i1 and i2. The magnitude of the magnetic induction at a point P at a distance d from the point O in a direction perpendicular to the plane ABCD is
questions.explanation: B1 = μ0i1/2πd, B2 = μ0i2/2πd, they are perpendicular. B = √(B12 + B22) = μ0/2πd √(i12 + i22)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current of i ampere is flowing in an equilateral triangle of side a. The magnetic induction at the centroid will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square frame of side l carries a current i. The magnetic field at its centre is B. The same current is passed through a circular coil having the same perimeter as the square. The field at the centre of the circular coil is B'. The ratio of B'/B is
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic induction at the point O, if the wire carries a current i, is
questions.explanation: B due to straight wires at O = (μ0i/4πR) * 2 * sin90? Actually for infinite wire, B = μ0i/2πR, but here they are semi-infinite, so B_straight = μ0i/4πR each, in opposite directions. The net from the two straight wires is μ0i/2πR in one direction. B_semicircle = μ0i/4R. The two are perpendicular. So B_net = √ = μ0i/4R √ = μ0i/4R √(4/π2 + 1) = μ0i/4R √((4+π2)/π2) = μ0i/4πR √(4+π2). This matches option (3).

questions.explanation: Standard result: M/L = q/2m

questions.explanation: B_centre = μ0nI, B_end = (1/2)μ0nI B_centre : B_end = 2 : 1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A conducting circular loop of radius r carries a constant current i. It is placed in uniform magnetic field B, such that B is perpendicular to the plane of the loop. The net magnetic force acting on the loop is
questions.explanation: Net force on a closed loop in uniform magnetic field will always be zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A circular loop of radius R is kept in a uniform magnetic field pointing perpendicular into the plane of paper. When a current I flows in the loop, the tension produced in the loop is
questions.explanation: Consider a small element. The magnetic force on the element is dF = I dl B, acting radially outward. The tension T in the loop balances this force. 2T sin(dθ/2) = I (R dθ) B. For small angles, sin(dθ/2) ≈ dθ/2. So T dθ = I R B dθ ⇒ T = I B R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If two straight current carrying wires are kept perpendicular to each other almost touching, then the wires
questions.explanation: Due to wire AB, wire CD will experience torque due to which it will become parallel to AB.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Connecting wires carrying currents in opposite directions are twisted together in using electrical appliances in order to reduce
questions.explanation: Twisting cancels the magnetic field produced by the currents.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A rectangular loop carrying a current i is situated near a long straight wire such that the wire is parallel to one of the sides of the loop and is in the plane of the loop. If a steady current i is established in the wire, the loop will

options.A: Rotate about an axis parallel to the wire

questions.explanation: Force on the two parallel sides will be attractive and repulsive respectively. The net force will be away from the wire.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square loop of side l is kept in a uniform magnetic field B such that its plane makes an angle α with B. The loop carries a current i. The torque experienced by the loop in this position is
questions.explanation: Torque = M × B, M = iA = i l2. The angle between M and B is 90-α. So τ = i l2 B sin(90-α) = i l2 B cos α

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The effective radius of a circular coil is R and number of turns is N. The current through it is i ampere. The work done in rotating the coil by angle of 180° in an external magnetic field B will be (initially plane of coil is perpendicular to magnetic field)
questions.explanation: W = -MB(cos180 - cos0) = -MB(-1 - 1) = 2MB = 2 * (NiπR2) * B = 2πNiR2B

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The current sensitivity of a moving coil galvanometer increases by 20% when its resistance is doubled. Calculate, by what factor does the voltage sensitivity change?

options.A: Increases the speed of the particle
options.B: Decreases the kinetic energy of the particle
options.C: Changes the direction of motion of the particle

questions.explanation: Magnetic force F ⟂ V, so no work is done by magnetic field so speed and kinetic energy cannot be changed by magnetic field but it can deflect the particle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In which of the following situations, the magnetic field can accelerate a charge particle at rest? I. When the magnetic field is uniform with respect to time as well as position II. When the magnetic field is time varying but uniform w.r.t. position III. When the magnetic field is time independent but position dependent
questions.explanation: When magnetic field is time varying, an induced electric field is produced which can accelerate charge.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of charge –q and mass m enters a uniform magnetic field B at A with speed v1 at an angle α and leaves the field at C with speed v2 at an angle β as shown. Then

options.C: Particle remains in the field for time t = 2m(π-α)/qB

questions.explanation: β = α, v1 = v2, and time inside field = (2π - 2α)/ω = 2(π-α)/ω = 2m(π-α)/qB

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton moving with a constant velocity, passes through a region of space without change in its velocity. If E & B represent the electric and magnetic fields respectively, this region may have
questions.explanation: For no change in velocity, net force = 0. This can happen if E=0 and the particle moves parallel to B (so no magnetic force). If E≠0, then velocity must be perpendicular to E and B such that qE = qvB. This is possible. Option (1) is possible (B field parallel to velocity). Option (2) would cause acceleration. Option (3) would cause force only from E. Option (4) would have both forces. So the most general answer is (1) as it is a subset of possible conditions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of charge per unit mass α is released from origin with a velocity v = v0 î in a uniform magnetic field B = -B0 k̂. If the particle passes through (0, y, 0) then y is equal to
questions.explanation: q/m = α. The particle will move in a circle. The point (0,y,0) is at a distance y from origin. The radius of the circle is r = v0/(B0α). The particle will reach (0,y,0) when it has covered half the circle, so y = 2r = 2v0/(B0α).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton is accelerating in a cyclotron where the applied magnetic field is 2 T. If the potential gap is effectively 100 kV then how much revolutions the proton has to make between the “dees” to acquire a kinetic energy of 20 MeV?
questions.explanation: Energy gained per revolution = 2 * 100 keV = 200 keV = 0.2 MeV Number of revolutions = 20 MeV / 0.2 MeV = 100

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current i ampere flows in a circular arc of wire which subtends an angle 3π/2 radian at its center, whose radius is R. The magnetic field B at its center is
questions.explanation: B = (μ0i/4πR) * θ (in radians) θ = 3π/2 B = (μ0i/4πR) * (3π/2) = 3μ0i/8R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two long straight wires are placed along x-axis and y-axis. They carry current I1 and I2 respectively. The equation of locus of zero magnetic induction in the magnetic field produced by them is
questions.explanation: B due to wire on x-axis = μ0I1/2πy (perpendicular to plane, outwards) B due to wire on y-axis = μ0I2/2πx (perpendicular to plane, inwards) For zero, they must be equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. So μ0I1/2πy = μ0I2/2πx ⇒ I1/y = I2/x ⇒ y = (I1/I2)x

questions.explanation: In ampere circuital law, on amperian loop B is due to all the current elements either inside or outside to the amperian loop.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two moles of an ideal gas are contained within a cylinder by a frictionless piston and are initially at temperature T. The pressure of the gas remains constant while it is heated and its volume doubles. If R is molar gas constant, the work done by the gas in increasing its volume is
questions.explanation: W = PΔV = P(2V - V) = PV = nRT = 3RT

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two moles of a gas at temperature T and volume V are heated to twice its volume at constant pressure. If γ = Cp/Cv then increase in internal energy of the gas is
questions.explanation: ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW ΔQ - ΔW = ΔU W/γ-1 = ΔU ΔW = nRΔT = nR(2T - T) = nRT = 2RT ΔU = 2RT/γ-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A triatomic, diatomic and monatomic gas is supplied same amount of heat at constant pressure, then

options.A: Fractional energy used to change internal energy is maximum in monatomic gas
options.B: Fractional energy used to change internal energy is maximum in diatomic gas
options.C: Fractional energy used to change internal energy is maximum in triatomic gases
options.D: Fractional energy used to change internal energy is same in all the three gases

questions.explanation: ΔU/ΔQ = nCvΔT/nCpΔT = 1/γ γ_mono = 5/3, ΔU/ΔQ = 3/5 = 0.6 γ_dia = 7/5, ΔU/ΔQ = 5/7 ≈ 0.714 γ_tria = 4/3, ΔU/ΔQ = 3/4 = 0.75 Fractional energy used to change internal energy is maximum in Triatomic gas.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: 105 calories of heat is required to raise the temperature of 3 moles of an ideal gas at constant pressure from 30°C to 35°C. The amount of heat required in calories to raise the temperature of the gas through the range (60°C to 65°C) at constant volume is (γ = 1.4)
questions.explanation: At constant pressure heat absorbed = ΔQ = nCpΔT …(1) At constant volume heat absorbed = ΔU = nCvΔT …(2) Dividing (1) by (2), ΔQ/ΔU = Cp/Cv = γ = 1.4 105/ΔU = 1.4 ⇒ ΔU = 75 cal

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: To an ideal triatomic gas 800 cal heat energy is given at constant pressure. If vibrational mode is neglected, then energy used by gas in work done against surroundings is
questions.explanation: Heat at constant pressure ΔQ = nCpΔT Heat for doing work ΔW = nRΔT Then ΔW/ΔQ = R/Cp = 1-1/γ γ for triatomic (neglecting vibration) = 4/3 ΔW = 800 * (1 - 3/4) = 800 * 1/4 = 200 cal

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A closed cylindrical vessel contains N moles of an ideal diatomic gas at a temperature T. On supplying heat, temperature remains same, but n moles get dissociated into atoms. The heat supplied is
questions.explanation: Heat supplied = ΔU = Ufinal - Uinitial Initial internal energy = 5/2 NRT Final internal energy when 'n' moles get dissociated = 5/2 (N - n)RT + 3/2 (2n)RT Ufinal = 5/2 (N - n)RT + 3nRT ΔU = - 5/2 NRT = 5/2 NRT - 5/2 nRT + 3nRT - 5/2 NRT = nRT(3 - 5/2) = 1/2 nRT

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Figure shows the isotherms of a fixed mass of an ideal gas at three temperatures TA, TB and TC, then
questions.explanation: ∵ PV = RT P = RT/V For constant 1/V, So, P ∝ T ∵ PC > PB > PA then TC > TB > TA

questions.question: An ideal monatomic gas at 300 K expands adiabatically to 8 times its volume. What is the final temperature?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Slope of isotherm for a gas (having γ = 5/3) is 3 × 105 N/m2. If the same gas is undergoing adiabatic change then adiabatic elasticity at that instant is
questions.explanation: Adiabatic elasticity = γP = γ * (Isothermal Elasticity) P is the same. Slope of isotherm gives P? Actually, for an isothermal process, the bulk modulus is P. So P = 3 × 105 N/m2. Adiabatic elasticity = (5/3) * 3 × 105 = 5 × 105 N/m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A gas may expand either adiabatically or isothermally. A number of P–V curves are drawn for the two processes over different range of pressure and volume. It will be found that

options.A: An adiabatic curve and an isothermal curve may intersect
options.B: Two adiabatic curves do not intersect
options.C: Two isothermal curves do not intersect

questions.explanation: Slope for isothermal and adiabatic are not same so they will intersect.

questions.explanation: PVγ = constant P ∝ V–γ dP/dV = -γ P/V dP/dV = γ P/V At point A, P = 3P0, V = 2V0, γ = 5/3 |dP/dV| = (5/3) * (3P0)/(2V0) = 5P0/2V0

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: TVγ-1 = K log T + (γ-1) log V = 0 log T = - (γ-1) log V y = - (γ - 1) x Slope from graph = (4-2)/(2-1) = 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A cyclic process on an ideal monatomic gas is shown in figure. The correct statement is

options.A: Work done by gas in process AB is more than that in the process BC
options.B: Net heat energy has been supplied to the system
options.C: Temperature of the gas is maximum at state B
options.D: In process CA, heat energy is absorbed by system

questions.explanation: It is a cyclic system ⇒ ΔU = 0 and work done is (+)ive, so heat is supplied to system.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A diatomic gas undergoes a process represented by PV1.3 = constant. Choose the incorrect statement

options.A: The gas expands by absorbing heat from the surroundings
options.B: The gas cools down during expansion
options.C: The work done by surroundings during expansion of the gas is negative

questions.explanation: PV1.3 = K W = (P1V1 - P2V2)/(n-1) ∵ n > 1, so W is negative. Heat supplied by surrounding heat goes to do work. ∴ Down when expands.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The process CD is shown in the diagram. As system is taken from C to D, what happens to the temperature of the system?

options.A: Temperature first decreases and then increases
options.B: Temperature first increases and then decreases

questions.explanation: For a point on the curve, PV = constant for an isothermal. Here, at C: 3P0V0, at D: 3P0V0. So start and end are on the same isotherm. However, for points in between, PV > P0V0? Actually, for a curve that is less steep than an isothermal? This is a straight line from C to D. The product PV is maximum somewhere in the middle, hence temperature first increases then decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A P-T graph is shown for a cyclic process. Select correct statement regarding this

options.A: During process CD, work done by gas is negative
options.B: During process AB, work done by the gas is positive
options.C: During process BC internal energy of system increases
options.D: During process BC internal energy of the system decreases

questions.explanation: In process BC (isochoric process) where ΔT is (+)ive. So ΔU = nCvΔT ∵ ΔT is positive ⇒ U increases

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hydrogen cylinder is designed to withstand an internal pressure of 100 atm. At 27°C, hydrogen is pumped into the cylinder which exerts a pressure of 20 atm. At what temperature does the danger of explosion first sets in?
questions.explanation: Constant volume process PV = nRT P1/T1 = P2/T2 20/300 = 100/T2 T2 = 1500 K

questions.explanation: 5/2 R of diatomic = Cv? The graph P ∝ T implies V = constant (isochoric). Molar heat capacity for isochoric process is Cv. For diatomic gas, Cv = (5/2)R = 2.5 R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An ideal gas expands according to the law P2V = constant. The internal energy of the gas
questions.explanation: P2V = K or PV–2 = K? Actually P2V = K => P ∝ V-1/2. This is not a standard polytropic form PVn = K. Let's write it as P * V1/2 = K'? Not correct. Better: P2V = K => (nRT/V)2 * V = K => n2R2T2 / V = K => T2 ∝ V. So as V increases, T increases. Internal energy U ∝ T, so U increases continuously.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: 4.0 g of a gas occupies 22.4 litres at NTP. The specific heat capacity of the gas at constant volume is 5.0 JK–1 mol–1. If the speed of sound in this gas at NTP is 952 ms–1, then the heat capacity at constant pressure is (Take gas constant R = 8.3 JK–1 mol–1)
questions.explanation: Molecular weight M = mass/ no of moles? 4g at NTP occupies 22.4L, so it's 4g/mol? Actually, 22.4L at NTP is 1 mole, so 4g = 1 mole, so M = 4 g/mol. γ = Cp/Cv. Speed of sound v = √(γRT/M) 952 = √(γ * 8.3 * 273 / 0.004) γ = (9522 * 0.004) / (8.3 * 273) ≈ (906304 * 0.004) / 2265.9 ≈ 3625.2 / 2265.9 ≈ 1.6 Cp = γ * Cv = 1.6 * 5 = 8.0 J/K/mol.

questions.question: An ideal gas is compressed to half its initial volume by means of several processes. Which of the process results in the maximum work done on the gas?
questions.explanation: Work done on the gas is maximum for adiabatic compression. On a PV diagram, the area under the curve is maximum for the adiabatic path.

questions.question: Figure below shows two paths that may be taken by a gas to go from a state A to a state C. In process AB, 400 J of heat is added to the system and in process BC, 100 J of heat is added to the system. The heat absorbed by the system in the process AC will be
questions.explanation: In process ABC ∵ ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW So, ΔU = ΔQ – ΔW ΔU = (400 + 100) – ΔU = 500 – 120 ΔU = 380 J In process AC ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW = 380 + = 380 + = 380 + 80 = 460 J

questions.question: A gas is taken through the cycle A →B →C →A, as shown. What is the net work done by the gas?
questions.explanation: ∵ Cyclic curve is clockwise i.e., W = +ve W = area enclosed = 1/2 * 5 * 10^-3 * 4 * 10^5 = 1/2 * 5 * 4 * 10^2 = 1/2 * 20 * 100 = 1000 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The molar specific heats of an ideal gas at constant pressure and volume are denoted by Cp and Cv respectively. If γ = Cp/Cv and R is the universal gas constant, then Cv is equal to
questions.explanation: Cp - Cv = R Cp/Cv = γ So γCv - Cv = R Cv(γ-1) = R Cv = R/(γ-1)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: During an adiabatic process, the pressure of a gas is found to be proportional to the cube of its temperature. The ratio of Cp/Cv for the gas is:
questions.explanation: P ∝ T3 PT-3 = constant For an adiabatic process, P1-γ Tγ = constant. So, γ/(1-γ) = -3 γ = -3(1-γ) => γ = -3 + 3γ => -2γ = -3 => γ = 3/2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the given (V – T) diagram, what is the relation between pressures P1 and P2?
questions.explanation: ∵ PV = RT V = (R/P) T Slope of V-T graph = R/P θ2 > θ1 so tanθ2 > tanθ1 ⇒ R/P2 > R/P1 ⇒ 1/P2 > 1/P1 ⇒ P2 < P1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A thermodynamic system is taken through the cycle ABCD as shown in figure. Heat rejected by the gas during the cycle is
questions.explanation: Heat rejected = workdone by surrounding = area of PV graph = P × 2V = 2PV

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An ideal gas goes from state A to state B via three different processes as indicated in the P-V diagram If Q1, Q2, Q3 indicate the heat absorbed by the gas along the three processes and ΔU1, ΔU2, ΔU3 indicate the change in internal energy along the three processes respectively, then

options.B: Q3 > Q2 > Q1 and ΔU1 = ΔU2 = ΔU3
options.C: Q1 = Q2 = Q3 and ΔU1 > ΔU2 > ΔU3
options.D: Q3 > Q2 > Q1 and ΔU1 > ΔU2 > ΔU3

questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: During an isothermal expansion, a confined ideal gas does –150 J of work against its surroundings. This implies that

options.A: 150 J of heat has been added to the gas
options.B: 150 J of heat has been removed from the gas
options.C: 300 J of heat has been added to the gas
options.D: No heat is transferred because the process is isothermal

questions.explanation: It implies 150 J heat has been removed from the gas.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A mass of diatomic gas (γ = 1.4) at a pressure of 2 atmospheres is compressed adiabatically so that its temperature rises from 27°C to 927°C. The pressure of the gas in the final state is
questions.explanation: For adiabatic process, P1-γ Tγ = constant. P(1-γ)/γ ∝ T? Better use P1-γ Tγ = constant. (P2/P1)1-γ = (T1/T2)γ (P2/2)1-1.4 = (300/1200)1.4 (P2/2)-0.4 = (1/4)1.4 (P2/2)0.4 = 41.4 = 4(7/5) = (22)(7/5) = 214/5 = 22.8 Taking both sides to the power 5/2: P2/2 = (22.8)5/2 = 27 = 128 P2 = 256 atm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If ΔU and ΔW represent the increase in internal energy and work done by the system respectively in a thermodynamical process, which of the following is true?

options.A: ΔU = – ΔW, in a isothermal process
options.B: ΔU = – ΔW, in a adiabatic process
options.C: ΔU = ΔW, in a isothermal process
options.D: ΔU = ΔW, in a adiabatic process

questions.explanation: In adiabatic process Q = 0 So ΔU = – ΔW

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If Cp and Cv denote the specific heats (per unit mass) of an ideal gas of molecular weight M, where R is the molar gas constant
questions.explanation: Cp - Cv = R/M Because Cp & Cv are given per unit mass And Cp – Cv = R is for 1 mole So here we use R/M where M is molecular mass.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A monoatomic gas at pressure P1 and V1 is compressed adiabatically to 1/8 th its original volume. What is the final pressure of the gas?
questions.explanation: ∵ PVγ = constant P2 = P1 (V1/V2)γ γ for monoatomic = 5/3 P2 = P1 * (8)5/3 = P1 * (23)5/3 = P1 * 25 = 32 P1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In thermodynamic processes which of the following statements is not true?

options.A: In an isochoric process pressure remains constant
options.B: In an isothermal process the temperature remains constant
options.C: In an adiabatic process PV γ = constant
options.D: In an adiabatic process the system is insulated from the surroundings

questions.explanation: In isochoric processes volume remains constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The internal energy change in a system that has absorbed 2 Kcals of heat and done 500 J of work is
questions.explanation: 1 Kcal = 4184 J approx. 2 Kcals = 8368 J ΔU = ΔQ - ΔW = 8368 - 500 = 7868 J ≈ 7900 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If Q, E and W denote respectively the heat added, change in internal energy and the work done in a closed cycle process, then

questions.question: A: Every metal has a definite work function, still all photoelectrons do not come out with the same energy if incident radiation is monochromatic. Not all electrons in the metal belong to this level rather they occupy a continuous band of levels.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.question: A: Work function of aluminium is 4.2eV . Emission of electrons will be possible by two photons, each of 2.5eV energy, striking the electron of aluminium. R: Energy of a photon can be less than the work function of the metal, for photoelectron emission.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Sol. Answer (4) Both the assertion and reason are incorrect. The quantised nature of light allows emission only when the photon striking the electron has enough energy to eject the electron. There will be no emission due to photons with energy less than work function.

questions.explanation: Sudden decrease in current due to circuit breaking (opening) will be compensated by sudden induced current flow and hence sudden brightness.

questions.explanation: Using Lenz's law, current in A will be clockwise.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A positive charge q moves along the line AB, which lies in the same plane as a circular loop of conducting wire as shown in the figure. The direction of current induced in the loop is [Diagram of charge moving near a loop]
questions.explanation: As the charge approaches, flux increases, induced current is clockwise to oppose. As it moves away, flux decreases, induced current is anticlockwise.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Figure shows an L-R circuit. When the switch S is closed, the current through resistor R1, R2 and R3 are I1, I2 and I3 respectively. The value of I1, I2 and I3 at t = 0 s is [Circuit with inductors and resistors]
questions.explanation: At t = 0, inductor offers infinite resistance, so no current in branches with inductors. So I1 = E/R1, I2 = 0, I3 = 0.

options.A: Both the coils have same magnitude of magnetic flux
options.B: The magnetic flux linked are in the ratio φA/φB = NA/NB
options.C: The induced emf across each coil are in the ratio EA/EB = (NA/NB)2
options.D: Both the coils have same magnitude of induced emf

questions.explanation: φA/φB = NA/NB

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A simple pendulum with bob of mass m and conducting wire of length L swings under gravity through an angle 2θ. The earth's magnetic field component in the direction perpendicular to swing is B. The maximum potential difference induced across the pendulum is [Diagram of a pendulum swing]
questions.explanation: vmax = √(2gL(1-cosθ)) = 2√(gL) sin(θ/2) εmax = (1/2)BvmaxL = (1/2)B × 2√(gL) sin(θ/2) × L = BL√(gL) sin(θ/2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The switch shown in the circuit is closed at t = 0. The current drawn from the battery by the circuit at t = 0 and t = ∞ are in the ratio [Circuit with inductor and capacitor]
questions.explanation: At t = 0, L offers infinite resistance, C offers zero resistance. At t → ∞, L offers zero resistance, C offers infinite resistance. In both cases, the effective resistance is the same, so current ratio is 1:1.

questions.question: A uniform magnetic field exists in the region given by B = 3i + 4j + 5k. A rod of length 5 m placed along y-axis is moved along x-axis with constant speed 1 ms–1. Then induced e.m.f. in the rod is
questions.explanation: ε = (v × B)·l v = 1i, B = 3i + 4j + 5k, l = 5j v × B = i × (3i + 4j + 5k) = 4k - 5j (v × B)·l = (4k - 5j)·5j = -25 Magnitude = 25 V

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A copper rod AB of length l, pivoted at one end A, rotates at constant angular velocity ω, at right angles to a uniform magnetic field of induction B. The emf, developed between the mid point C of the rod and end B is [Diagram of rotating rod]
questions.explanation: εCB = ∫(l/2 to l) Bωx dx = Bω [x2/2]l/2l = Bω(l2/2 - l2/8)/2? Let's do properly: ε = ∫ Bωx dx from x = l/2 to l = Bω [x2/2]l/2l = Bω/2 (l2 - l2/4) = Bω/2 × 3l2/4 = 3Bωl2/8

questions.explanation: Total energy U = Q2/2C Energy in capacitor at charge Q/2 = (Q/2)2/2C = Q2/8C = U/4 Energy in inductor = U - U/4 = 3U/4

What is the rate of heat dissipation when the square wave potential difference V2 as shown in figure is applied across the resistor? [Two graphs: sine wave and square wave]
questions.explanation: For sine wave, Vrms = V0/√2, so W = (V0/√2)2/R = V02/2R For square wave, Vrms = V0, so W' = V02/R = 2W

questions.subject: Physics

options.C: As the frequency is increased, brightness of B1 will increase and that of B2 will decrease
options.D: As the frequency is decreased, the brightness of B1 will increase and that of B2 will decrease

questions.explanation: As frequency is increased, XC decreases and XL increases, so brightness of B1 will increase and that of B2 will decrease.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: Total energy = Q02/2C When UC = 3UL, then UC + UL = total ⇒ 3UL + UL = total ⇒ 4UL = total UL = total/4 = Q02/8C = (1/2)LI2 I2 = Q02/(4LC) ⇒ I = Q0/(2√(LC))

questions.explanation: For square pulse (d) with equal on/off time? Actually, for a DC signal, peak = rms. For a square wave with equal positive and negative halves, Vrms = Vpeak.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two cables of copper are of equal lengths. One of them has a single wire of area of cross-section A, while other has 10 wires of cross-sectional area A/10 each. Give their suitability for transporting A.C. and D.C.

options.A: Only multiple strands for A.C, either for D.C.
options.B: Only multiple strands for A.C, only single strand for D.C.
options.C: Only single strand for D.C, either for A.C.
options.D: Only single strand for A.C, either for D.C.

questions.explanation: Multiple strands reduce skin effect for AC. For DC, both are suitable.

questions.question: In an electrical circuit R,L,C and an a.c. voltage source are all connected in series. When L is removed from the circuit, the phase difference between the voltage and the current in the circuit is π/3. If instead, C is removed from the circuit, the phase difference is again π/3. The power factor of the circuit is
questions.explanation: When L removed, tan φ = XC/R = tan(π/3) = √3 When C removed, tan φ = XL/R = √3 So XL = XC, hence resonance. Power factor = 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A capacitor and a bulb are connected in series with a source of alternating emf. If a dielectric slab is inserted between the plates of the capacitor, then

options.A: The brightness of the bulb decreases
options.B: The brightness of the bulb increases
options.C: The brightness of the bulb remains same
options.D: The brightness of the bulb becomes zero

questions.explanation: Inserting dielectric increases C, decreases XC, so current increases, bulb brightness increases.

questions.explanation: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np = 5000/500 = 10 Vs = 10 × 20 = 200 V, frequency unchanged (50 Hz)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is the value of inductance L for which the current is maximum in a series LCR circuit with C = 10 μF and ω = 1000 s–1?

options.B: Cannot be calculated unless R is known

questions.explanation: For maximum current, XL = XC ωL = 1/(ωC) ⇒ L = 1/(ω2C) = 1/((1000)2 × 10-5) = 1/(10) = 0.1 H = 100 mH

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of self inductance L is connected in series with a bulb and an A.C. source. Brightness of the bulb decreases when

options.B: A capacitance of reactance XC = XL is included in the same circuit
options.C: An iron rod is inserted in the coil
options.D: Frequency of the A.C. source is decreased

questions.explanation: Inserting iron rod increases L, increases XL, decreases current, so brightness decreases.

questions.explanation: At t=0, inductors act as open, capacitors as short. So I1 = 0, I2 = E/R, I3 = E/(2R). So I2 > I3 > I1.
